rulings.Case Title: EST. OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order: (1) Removing Trustee; (2) Compelling Trustee To Account; (3) Reduction Of Trustee Compensation; (4) Barring Trustee?S Use Of Trust Funds For Litigation; And (5) Awarding Damages And Attorneys? Fees And Costs For Financial Elder Abuse
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any supporting papers ave not been received by the assigned examiner as of 1/6/23. As this petition appears to be identical to the one filed on the testamentary trust case, the Court will likely take this petition off calendar if not withdrawn/dismissed by Petitioners. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/1/23. (No earlier available date.) Proof of payment of the assessment fee in the related case must be filed before an accounting can be approved. Bond was increased by $21,000. Reimbursements to a fiduciary must show who the original payee was. The "Declaration" supplementing the petition with parties entitled to notice was not signed and verified by the co-trustees. [CRC 7.103(c).] (Maurice Ward was appointed successor trustee on 6/22/11, not 7/9/11.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VALENTINE FAMILY LIVING DATED MAY 13, 1994 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/1/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Under what authority did the trustee make distributions to the minor for the minor beneficiary for his birthday, "living expenses", and school tuition. Are those not a parent's responsibility? For what services did the trustee pay legal fees to an immigration attorney? The number of shares of the two CDs held with Merrill Lynch appear on the statement to be 162,000, not 1,000, as shown on Schedule E of the accounting. Please explain the $810 "return of difference" of Angel Oak FINL INSTS shown on 10/24/21 on Schedule F of the accounting (p. 22 of 26). (This should have been filed in a new SEPARATE trust case.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 2/17/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH H. HOPKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continue to 01/24/2023 by the judicial officer after review. The following issues must be addressed: 1) Does the last paragraph of the decedent's first codicil require direct distribution of the partial net proceeds from the sale of the Arroyo property ($94,404) to the beneficiaries named in Article 7, Paragraph B of the devisee trust, as amended, rather than distribution to the trustee as prayed? 2) The 2012 amendment to the decedent's trust deleted Article 7, Paragraph C, which was the residuary beneficiary clause. Does the distribution of assets, other than the Arroyo property proceeds received from Alicia's estate ($94,404), to the devisee trust fail, as there are no longer recipients as the residual beneficiaries of the trust after the 2012 amendment? If so, should the assets be distributed to a testamentary trust created by the savings provision, incorporating the terms of the 2009 trust including the residuary beneficiary clause, without giving effect to the 2012 amendment?

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHIRLEY CHONG 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To (1) Remove Albert Chong As Successor Trustee Of The Shirley Chong 2016 Trust, (2) Order Any Successor Trustee To Comply With The Terms
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2023 (no earlier date available), as the following issues e-mailed on 09/13/2022 remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form with a proof of service is on file. A copy of the most recent remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Re each minor beneficiary identified in paragraph VII of the petition: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. iii) No guardian guardian ad litem has been appointed for any minor beneficiary. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/15/2023 (no earlier date available): 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 2) Re each minor beneficiary entitled to notice: i) The date of birth must be provided. ii) All the persons having legal custody of and residing with the minor entitled to notice under CRC 7.51(d) must be identified. iii) No guardian ad litem has been appointed for any minor beneficiary. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BRIAN WISHNEFSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUANG MEI LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 8/3/17. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 1/24/24. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 1/24/24, no appearance will be required on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELLA MAE FORD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/8/2023. The following procedural issues must be cleared two weeks prior: REGARDING PETITION: (1) Need to re-notice. Notice filed on 12/22/2022 failed to set forth Petition seeks admission of Lost Will. CRC 7.50. (2) Need to re-publish as the published notice failed to set forth Lost Will being offered to probate. CRC 7.54. (3) Need further allegation or clarification under which ground Petition seeks the lost will to be admitted. Appears PC 6124 rebuttable presumption of revocation may be applicable based on the petitioner's allegation; however, Petition does not show how the presumption can be rebutted. Also, Petition does not provide any information of any efforts to retrieve the original will from the decedent's sister. This petition seems premature for hearing. (4) Proposed Order to include a copy of lost will. PC 8223. (5) Need to identify the trustee of the Rivers & Ella 2016 Trust. (5) If the petitioner is the sole trustee of said trust, Petition to identify the trust beneficiaries who must be served with Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121]. PC 1208. Without the information, examiner unable to review if the PC 8110 notice requirements are met. REGARDING OBJECTION: (1) Need Proof of Service of the objection. (2) Need further allegation of the ground of the opposition. Objectors allege that the decedent's real property has been titled in the decedent's mother's trust, thus there is no need to commence this decedent's estate case. However, the Lara Mae Ford Family Trust is not subject to this proceeding.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BABETTE BARTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/27/23. (No earlier available date.) No proposed order has been submitted or received yet by the assigned examiner as of 1/5/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies of the petition or any supporting papers, other than the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number, have been received yet by the assigned examiner as of 1/5/23. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/16/23, to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
